one_time_description: Gain on settlement of accounts payable totalling $2,899,757, resulting in a gain of $815,923
economic_notes: Nation is attempting to develop alternate sources of revenue to become self-sufficient. COVID-19 impacted operations. Multiplex project under construction with $3,200,000 Peace Hills Trust loan and $1,196,000 First Peoples Economic Growth Fund approved but not yet drawn.
notes: Audit is qualified on two matters: (1) Sandy Bay ASETS - incomplete financial reporting process affecting payables, deferred revenue, and program expenditures; (2) Sandy Bay Smoke Shop - incomplete records affecting revenues, expenses, receivables and payables. Prior year restated to consolidate Sandy Bay Housing Authority Inc. which had not previously been included. 11 fully consolidated subsidiaries. No schedule of remuneration provided. Restricted cash includes replacement reserve ($308,048), CMHC restricted funds ($593,793), and restricted capital funds ($2,830,757). First Peoples Development Inc revenue of $1,491,043 appears in 2020 but not as a named line in segment totals - included in other revenue. Province of Manitoba $358,839 also included in other revenue in segment disclosure.
